headline: New Stadium Ticket Prices Enrage Bills Faithful
writeup: The opening of the Buffalo Bills' new $2.1 billion Highmark Stadium has sparked outrage among fans, with the cheapest seats starting at $700 and supporters across social media calling the prices 'outrageous' and 'scary.' Meanwhile, Josh Allen continues to dominate the offseason narrative, speaking candidly about the team's new-look roster, the critical importance of training camp, and his confidence in new weapons like DJ Moore, who he says will be a 'huge help.' New defensive back C.J. Gardner-Johnson made headlines by guaranteeing a Super Bowl win alongside Allen, while also addressing past locker room concerns head-on. On the personnel front, questions swirl around which defenders fit Jim Leonhard's new defensive scheme, whether recent Bills star Joey Bosa is retiring, and the contract status of guard O'Cyrus Torrence. Despite some damning offensive outlooks from analysts, the Bills' offseason energy remains high as training camp approaches.
